title: Agartha
summary: The subterranean kingdom — six trillion inhabitants, an ancient war with Atlantis, and a council that still rules from below.
markdown:
**Agartha** — the realm within the Earth — is far more in the archive than a rumor of tunnels.
The April 7, 2020 census gives its scale: **six trillion Agarthians**, making the world below
vastly more populous than the world above. Its people appear throughout the sessions as
neighbors just out of reach — "how could I contact Agarthians or Lyrans?" — and its councils
as active powers in the planet's governance.

Agartha's deep history is martial. The September 4, 2020 [Atlantis](/lore/atlantis/) session
names the **Agartha Kingdom of Rama** as one side of the space-fleet war that ended the golden
age around 15,300 BC — the cataclysm that collapsed the ice shield and shrank humanity. The
kingdom survived the war it helped fight, and the archive keeps it in play ever after: the
July 13, 2020 session has the **Council of Agartha** authorizing the geopolitical
restructuring of the surface world, and the December 2023 [NESARA](/lore/nesara-gesara/)
session describes a royal family sheltered "down below… Agartha," its vaults tied to the
promised wealth transfer.

Together with the [Inner and Hollow Earth](/lore/inner-earth/) material — the central sun, the
Inner Earth Councils — Agartha completes the archive's vertical cosmology: as much history
beneath our feet as above our heads, and older thrones than any on the surface.
